title: Duno Dree
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Dree}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:DunoDreeRes.png]] |name=Duno Dree |homeworld= |birth=[[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]]''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' gives Duno Dree an age of seventeen at the time of the [[mission to Dorlo]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''Rebel Agent'' to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Using simple math a date of [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]] can be surmised as Dree's date of birth. |death=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', and the [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. aboard the [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']] over [[Dorlo]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Red]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Dialogue|Jerec|Give me the coordinates.|Rahn|Tell them, Duno—tell them for both of us.|Dree|[''To Jerec''] "No.|attr=Dree defies Jerec for the last time|src=}} '''Duno Dree''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who accompanied the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dorlo]] during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Also known as '''Druno''', he originally worked for his [[Parent/Legends|father]], piloting in-system [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]]. Dree joined Rahn as the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] cobbled together a team that was to research the mythical [[Valley of the Jedi]] located on the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]. The other team members included the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] weapons expert [[Cee Norley]]—whom Dree secretly held affections for—, and the [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] [[Tech specialist|technologist]] [[Rolanda Gron]]. At some point during 5 ABY, Dree flew the team to the planet [[Dorlo]] in a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] in hopes of locating and hiring the linguistics expert [[Nij Por Ral]]. That part of the mission was successful, but they found themselves pursued by the forces of a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] splinter group led by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]. Dree attempted to fly the team off world, and destroyed an Imperial [[Skimmer/Legends|skimmer]] in the process. However, they were captured by Jerec's personal [[Starship/Legends|warship]], [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']]. Jerec demanded that he be given the location of the mythical valley, and threatened a painful [[Death/Legends|death]] if otherwise. However, the team members resisted, resulting in the executions of Gron and Por Ral. The Dark Jedi continued to push for information, leaving Dree to answer both for himself and on the behalf of Rahn: a determined "No." In response, Dree's neck was snapped by the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Dark Jedi [[Boc Aseca]], and the information that Jerec sought was [[Drain Knowledge|forcibly taken]] from Rahn's mind, and the Jedi was killed soon after. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|Druno—time to show us how good a pilot you really are!|Rahn|[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]}} Druno Dree was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] born during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]] to a man who ran a freight business. Dree worked for his [[Parent/Legends|father]] part-time, piloting in-system [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], becoming a somewhat skilled [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]] Dree was also enrolled in a [[school]] which lasted for at least six years. Within the six years Dree continued to work for his father, although his total time spent actually flying totaled three years. Dree sought adventure to a degree and wanted to have a tale to entertain his children with in the future. He eventually left his father's business by [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|fall]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. At some point he obtained a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and met a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] named [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]]. ===The Hunt for the Valley of the Jedi=== ====Mission to Dorlo==== {{Quote|We should have taken…taken the skimmer. I could have flown us back and forth to our ship five times by now."
"Spoken like a true pilot, my young friend Druno.|Duno Dree and Rahn|}} [[File:Qurahn.png|thumb|right|150px|Jedi Master Rahn]] The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master Rahn had learned of the location of a mythical location called the [[Valley of the Jedi]], and had decided to assemble a team to investigate the valley, located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]. By 5 ABY, Dree had joined Rahn's team, which also consisted of a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] weapons expert named Cee Norley and a [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] [[Tech specialist|technologist]] named [[Rolanda Gron]]. By this point, Dree had begun to claim that he was twenty years old, even though he was actually seventeen, and also claimed that he was more of an exemplary pilot than he actually was. Rahn had also taken to calling him "Druno." In addition, Dree developed an emotional attachment to Norley, a wire-thin female that he came to care deeply for. However, he was afraid to admit his feelings to her. Rahn's team needed one more member: an expert at ancient linguistics named [[Nij Por Ral]]. The linguist was on the planet [[Dorlo]], where he was working on translating writing carved into ancient ruins uncovered by [[Mining/Legends|miners]] working for the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]]. Rahn wanted to recruit Por Ral so that he could help decipher any glyphs that were found in the Valley of the Jedi. The team made it to Dorlo, landed, and camouflaged Dree's [[Duno Dree's transport|transport]]. What Dree considered to be a lark mission, however, quickly turned into a life or death struggle. They had been pursued by agents of the Empire, led by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]], Rahn's adversary and rival in finding the lost valley. Rahn and the team managed to locate Por Ral and convinced him to join them on their quest. Using a [[Skimmer/Legends|skimmer]], the rebel team began their return journey to the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]]. At some point, they had to abandon the skimmer to continue their trek on foot. The group eventually came to a halt in order to catch their breath, at which point Dree complained that they should have continued to use the skimmer, and that he could have flown them to the ship and back several times already. Rahn commended Dree for his self-esteem, but explained that if they had used the skimmer, the Empire would track them via the skimmer's heat signature and it would all be over. Norley complained that the others weren't up the task, despite their objections, and mentioned that Dree wasn't up to the task because of his young age. With time running out, Rahn devised a plan that would have Norley stay behind and buy the others time to escape. ====Captured by dark forces==== {{Quote|Alright, lets skip all the preflight. Either this baby is gonna fly right now or she never will.|Dree as he prepares to take off|}} [[File:Dark_Forces_Corvette.png|thumb|left|275px|Dree's corvette is destroyed by the ''Vengeance''.]] With Norley behind, the rest of the team made it back to the corvette. They stripped it of camouflage and boarded it as Dree took to the controls. To speed up the escape, Dree cut half of the takeoff procedures. Concerned about Norley, he did not believe that she should be left behind. Rahn urged the boy to leave the [[Star system/Legends|system]] or else Norley's actions would have been done in vain. Realizing Norley's sacrifice (she managed to destroy a pursuing Imperial skimmer before being [[Death/Legends|killed]]), Dree found the last Imperial skimmer and fired upon it. Although no shot connected, he flew low enough over the skimmer to catch it in the slipstream, which caused the skimmer to crash, though most of its occupants survived. Dree left Dorlo's atmosphere, but encountered [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] and Jerec's [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']]. Dree attempted to avoid the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], not realizing that he was being coerced into a trap. Dree's corvette was caught in the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]], with the young pilot in vain struggling to dampen the runaway power plants; the indication that an [[Assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]] was coming to them, made the young pilot wish that he was back home with his family. Rahn realized that they were about to be boarded, at which point Dree promised to not go down without putting up a fight. An assault [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] docked with the corvette, and a [[Commando/Legends|commando]] team led by Jerec's Dark Jedi follower [[Boc Aseca]] attempted to coerce the crew off of the ship. After sixty seconds of waiting the [[Imperial commando/Legends|Imperial commandos]] filled the corvette with [[coma gas]]. Rahn tried to get everyone in protective suits, but the gas took its effect and knocked everyone aboard the ship unconscious. The imperials loaded their bodies onto stretchers and took them back to the ''Vengeance''. Dree's corvette was destroyed by the gunnery crews aboard the warship, who used the ship as target practice. ====Defiance==== {{Quote|Provide the coordinates for the planet, or the location where the coordinates can be found, and die a merciful death. Deny my request, and the suffering will last a long time.|Jerec's offer for the rebels|}} When Dree regained conscious, he and the others were marched to the ship's [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] as an orderly listed off military jargon. Dree and the others were handed off to the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sysco]], who informed Jerec that their prisoners were ready for interrogation. Jerec, surrounded by his [[Seven Dark Jedi|six Dark Jedi]] followers, met with the rebels and mockingly assumed that Dree and the others were Rahn's servants. Gron spoke up, stating his name and that he was not going to divulge any information. The Dark Jedi called [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]], who had been perched on the shoulder of his symbiotic twin [[Gorc]], lunged at Gron and stabbed the Klatooinian in the throat with a [[Dagger/Legends|dagger]] and rode the body to the floor. With the stakes clear, Jerec gave the others his demands: Tell him the location of the valley and be granted a merciful death, or suffer. Jerec was denied, and threatened the life of Por Ral via the Dark Jedi [[Yun]]. Rahn chose death, and Por Ral was executed, leaving Dree and Rahn as the last ones standing. Shaking from fear, tears streamed from Dree's eyes. When Jerec asked again for information regarding the valley, Dree looked to Rahn and the two locked [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Rahn told Druno to answer for the both of them. Dree eyes seemed to grow larger as he stood straight, faced Jerec, and gave the Dark Jedi a determined answer: "No." With his fate sealed, Dree shut his eyes and awaited death. Aseca stepped forward, getting close enough to Dree for the young pilot to smell the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek's]] breath. Aseca hands darted out and broke Dree's neck, which sent the body sprawling forward onto the ground. After Dree's death, Jerec [[Drain Knowledge|forcibly ripped the information]] he sought from Rahn's mind, and after a short-lived last stand from the Jedi, Rahn was [[Sai cha/Legends|beheaded]] by Jerec. Despite Jerec's promise of suffering in return for defiance, Aseca's execution killed Dree instantly. Dree and the others were avenged when the rebel agent [[Kyle Katarn]] fought Jerec at the Valley of the Jedi and defeated the Dark Jedi once and for all. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|What about Norley?''"
"''Never mind about her.''"
"''But we just can't leave her!''"
"''The more we talk the more we risk making her sacrifice one in vain.|Dree and Rahn argue about Norley's fate|}} [[File:Battle_of_Dorlo.png|thumb|right|250px|The ''Vengeance'' over Dorlo after capturing Dree and his allies]] Duno Dree was a confident young pilot who tended to lie about certain things that were about himself. Although he was seventeen during his last mission with Rahn, he claimed to be twenty years of age. He also knew that he was only half the pilot that he claimed to be. However, Rahn appreciated Dree's bold statements, and told him on one occasion that such speak was that of a true pilot. Rahn also considered Dree, whom he occasionally called Druno, a friend. Despite his bold piloting claims, Dree was unable to admit to Norley how deeply he cared for her. When Norley split from the group to buy the group time, Dree became concerned with her fate after they made it the corvette. After concluding that Norley was already dead by that point, Dree believed that the Empire would pay. In a brash move, he found the skimmer that hosted Norley's killers and attempted to destroy it with [[Cannon/Legends|cannon]] fire. However, he missed every shot. He still managed to destroy the Imperial skimmer, though, as it had been caught in his slipstream. When his ship was caught in a tractor beam, Dree held no illusions about his chances, and reflected that it was too late to tell the truth, tell Norley how he felt about her, and too late to remain in his father's business. Although he had flown for his father for three years, Dree did not believe it was enough experience to escape the Imperial ships that attacked him as he made it off of Dorlo. This was proven as he allowed TIE fighters to coerce him into a carefully laid trap that only Rahn managed to point out after it was too late. Dree touted bravery, vowing to fight any Imperials who tried to board his ship. He was never able to do such a thing, though, as everyone was knocked out with gas before the boarding commenced. On the bridge of the ''Vengeance'', Dree attempted to counsel Rahn, stating that, for the time being, they were still alive. Upon seeing Jerec for the first time, Dree questioned who the man was while describing his appearance. As the session continued, Dree grew visibly and emotionally shaken as his body shook and as tears rolled down his cheeks. When confronted with the option of giving in to Jerec's demands or holding out, he looked to Rahn, who told him answer for the both of them. Dree's voice was filled with determination as he denied the Dark Jedi for the last time. Although Jerec could not physically see Dree, due to being a naturally blind [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]], he sensed the boy's determination. Dree was a human male with dark-red [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that he kept in a crew-cut, freckles on his [[Nose/Legends|nose]], and peach-fuzz on both cheeks. He also had a scar on his forehead. ==Equipment== {{Quote|Well they won't get us without a fight. Not while I got a blaster handy.|Duno Dree|}} Dree piloted a CR90 corvette during the mission to Dorlo. The ship adequately suited the team's needs, with protective suits and forward [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. The [[blockade runner]] had red striping on the sides of the "head" and "abdomen" areas. It was destroyed by the ''Vengeance''{{'s}} gunnery crew. Dree also carried a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] during the mission, and planned to use it against the Imperials when they boarded his ship. However, he was knocked out with gas before that could happen. He wore orange [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] when he died. ==Behind the scenes== Duno Dree first appeared as unnamed character who appeared briefly in the first cutscene for the [[1997]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', where he was portrayed by an uncredited actor. The character's name and history were revealed in the [[1998]] novella ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', which was authored by [[William C. Dietz]]. Dree appeared in the book's second chapter as a supporting character with several point-of-view paragraphs, though he was only given a single line of dialogue. Dree also appeared in the full cast [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|audio adaption]], where his voice was provided by [[Michael Paul Levin]]. Dree was referred to as Druno throughout the audio drama, and was given significantly more lines of dialogue, expanding on the character's personality. Dree also received an entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' Dree is the only one of Rahn's companions to appear in all three versions of the ''Jedi Knight'' story. Between the different adaptions of ''Jedi Knight'', there are a few noteworthy discrepancies. Firstly, in the video game Dree has dark red hair, whereas the novella describes Dree as having dirty-blonde hair. Secondly, neither Dree or Nij Por Ral are executed in the audio adaption. Rather, Rolanda Gron is killed, and Rahn is beaten and killed soon after, leaving the fates of Dree and Por Ral ambiguous. This article assumes that Dree had red hair as seen in the game, seeing that the game has been the only source to depict an image of Dree. Due to the fact that Dree is killed in two of the three tellings, this article gives both the game and the novella precedence over the audio adaption, as both sources agree on the timing of Dree's death being right before Rahn's. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' {{1stID|Duno Dree}} *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] {{C|As Druno}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' {{1st}} {{C|Corpse only}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Duno Dree |pt=Duno Dree }} [[Category:Humans|Dree, Duno]] [[Category:Males|Dree, Duno]] [[Category:Pilots|Dree, Duno]]
